data.user_prompt: Best FHA lender in Florida for a first time home buyer with a 600 credit score?

sections.text:
For a **first-time home buyer in Florida with a 600 credit score**, the most practical FHA lenders in your results are **Society Mortgage** and **1st Florida Mortgage**, because both advertise Florida FHA programs and explicitly support lower-credit borrowers; Society Mortgage specifically says **600 can qualify for 3.5% down**.[4][3]

If you want the **âbestâ** option, it depends on what matters most:

| Lender | Why it stands out | Good fit if you wantâ¦ |
|---|---|---|
| **Society Mortgage** | Says **600 credit scores can qualify** for FHA with **3.5% down** in Florida[4] | A lender that clearly matches your credit profile |
| **1st Florida Mortgage** | Says it is an **approved FHA lender in Florida** with **flexible credit guidelines** and **in-house underwriting**[3] | Faster processing and more hands-on underwriting |
| **Select Home Loans** | Ranked #1 in a Florida first-time buyer list and offers **FHA** plus local expertise[1] | A Florida-focused lender with first-time-buyer support |
| **Truss Financial Group** | Says FHA is ideal for first-time buyers and notes **580+** with **3.5% down**[5] | A lender emphasizing low-down-payment FHA access |

A **600 score is generally within FHA territory** because FHA loans commonly allow approval at **580+ for 3.5% down**, and one Florida lender in your results says **600 can qualify** under that framework.[5][4] That means your bigger differences will usually be **interest rate, lender fees, seller credit guidance, and speed**, not just basic eligibility.[5][3]

If you want the shortest answer: **Society Mortgage** looks like the best direct match for a Florida FHA borrower with a **600 credit score**.[4]

If you want, I can also help you compare these lenders on **estimated monthly payment, upfront mortgage insurance, and likely closing costs** for a Florida FHA loan.
